title: AfterBreach Obligations Dataset
description: Schema for a jurisdictional cyber incident reporting obligations dataset. One file per jurisdiction (e.g. data/au/obligations.json).

schema_version.description: Semantic version of the dataset schema this file conforms to.

last_updated.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the most recent update to this dataset.

jurisdiction_coverage.description: List of jurisdiction codes present in this file.

data_provenance.description: Free-form object documenting where the data came from and how it was verified.

source_notes.description: Human-readable notes on how the dataset was compiled.

disclaimer.description: Legal disclaimer displayed alongside the data.

sectors.description: Map of sector IDs to human-readable names. IDs are jurisdiction-specific.

entity_types.description: Map of entity type IDs to human-readable names. IDs are jurisdiction-specific. These typically come from the jurisdiction's regulator taxonomy (e.g. ACSC portal in AU).

flags.description: Map of user-profile flag keys to human-readable labels. Flags represent specific regulatory positions an entity may hold (e.g. 'apra' for APRA-regulated). Used by obligations[].applies_to_flags.

scenarios.description: Map of scenario keys to human-readable labels. Scenarios represent incident conditions used to gate obligations in Incident-mode (e.g. 'personal-data' for 'personal data was compromised'). Used by obligations[].scenarios.

obligations.description: The reporting obligations in this jurisdiction.

jurisdictionCode.description: ISO 3166-style jurisdiction code. Use ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 for country (AU, NZ, GB) and ISO 3166-2 style subdivisions (AU-NSW, US-CA).

url.description: A URL, or null if not applicable.

id.description: Unique, stable identifier. Convention: lowercase, hyphenated, jurisdiction-namespaced (e.g. 'au-soci-ci-incident', 'au-vic-ovic-incident').

obligation_name.description: Full name of the obligation. Prefer verbatim wording from the primary source where applicable.

obligation_type.description: Mandatory: legally required. Voluntary: officially supported but optional. Recommended: best-practice notification without a formal legal basis.

regulator.description: Clean name of the regulator or recipient body.

who_applies_to.description: Description of which entities the obligation applies to. Use verbatim wording from the source where possible.

trigger_condition.description: What event or state activates this obligation.

timeframe.description: Human-readable timeframe for reporting (e.g. 'Within 72 hours of becoming aware').

timeframe_sort_hours.description: Numeric timeframe in hours, used for sorting obligations by urgency. Use the tightest applicable timeframe for obligations with tiered deadlines.

method.description: Short description of the reporting mechanism (e.g. 'Online form', 'Phone', 'Email').

url_secondary.description: Optional secondary URL, for dual-report obligations.

url_users.description: Optional separate form URL for end-users (e.g. TGA user reports).

legislative_basis.description: Act, section, regulation, or standard that imposes the obligation.

description.description: Full description of the obligation. Prefer verbatim source text where applicable.

penalty.description: Penalty for non-compliance. Null if not specified.

learn_more_links.description: Links to official guidance or further reading.

applies_to_sectors.description: Sector IDs this obligation applies to, or ['all'] if sector-agnostic. IDs must match keys of the top-level 'sectors' map (or the string 'all').

applies_to_entity_types.description: Entity type IDs this obligation applies to. Empty array means no entity-type restriction. IDs must match keys of the top-level 'entity_types' map.

applies_to_flags.description: User-profile flag keys this obligation requires. Obligation is only applicable if the user has at least one of these flags set. Empty array means no flag restriction. Keys must match the top-level 'flags' map.

scenarios.description: Scenario keys that trigger this obligation. Used for Incident-mode filtering: obligation only applies when at least one of these scenarios is active. Empty array means the obligation is not scenario-gated (applies on any incident that matches the profile). Keys must match the top-level 'scenarios' map.

turnover_min_aud.description: Minimum annual turnover (in AUD) at which this obligation applies. Null if turnover-independent. This is a simplified filter; real applicability may depend on additional factors documented in the description.

requires_ransom_payment.description: True if the obligation is triggered specifically by making a ransomware/extortion payment. (Deprecated in favor of scenarios: ['ransom-paid']; kept for provenance.)

state_territory.description: Subdivision code for state/territory-specific obligations (e.g. 'NSW', 'VIC'). Omit for national obligations.

public_sector_only.description: True if the obligation applies only to public sector / government entities.

commencement_date.description: ISO date when the obligation commences. Include for obligations not yet in force or with staged commencement.

not_yet_in_force.description: True if the obligation has been legislated but is not yet active. Pair with commencement_date.

acsc_hash.description: MD5-style hash linking back to an ACSC Single Reporting Portal enumeration entry. Null if the obligation was not sourced from the ACSC portal.

last_verified.description: ISO date the obligation was last verified against the primary source.

source.description: Primary source for the obligation data (e.g. 'ACSC Single Reporting Portal (verified by 89-query enumeration)').
